utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so dennis is a 57 -year-old male today complaining of shortness of breath he has also got a history of diabetes high blood pressure and history of asthma so dennis tell me what's going on how're you doing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not too good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay how long have you been feeling not too well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: about a couple of weeks

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: couple of weeks tell me what's going on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i wake up with shortness of breath

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i go to when i'm short of breath there i get back home and i'm again short of breath so i had a tough time dealing with this

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you noticed any are you coughing up anything any swelling in your legs or what's going on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i've been i've been coughing up i've been coughing up blood

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay how long has that been going on for

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: probably three weeks or so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and are you coughing a lot of blood or just a little bit of blood

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i shave three to four cups a day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow that's a lot of blood okay are you throwing up any blood are you having bleeding in any place else or just when you cough

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: only when i cough

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you noticed any bruising or bleeding from your teeth or anything like that or from your gums or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay any leg swelling or calf swelling or any any anything like that any recent travel history you've been on a plane or trip recently

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i had some knee problem but there is no swelling but it hurts that's been going on for quite some time

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so that's not near the knee problem okay and have you had any have you been to the hospital or been to the emergency room or anything like that for your coughing and coughing up blood

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i tried to get an appointment in the office but they were kinda booked up

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they just numb

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and alright no but no fevers with this so since you're here your oxygen level looks pretty good in the clinic it's about ninety four percent on pulse your pulse oxygenation so that's good are you short of breath so when you walk or you or you exert yourself you're short of breath you said right okay and have you and how how is your blood sugar been doing since this has been going on i know you're on metformin how's your diabetes been doing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not so good you know it kinda going up and down i wear this dexcom

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you know after meal they spike up well over two hundred

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and morning when i get up they are around one fifty

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so it's been running a little bit high i know we checked your hemoglobin a1c last time and it was about seven . seven . i think and we had talked about you know improving your diet and improving some exercise but is this blood sugar been high and low or high and normal when you're especially in the last three weeks or has it been going on for longer than that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would say about a month

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and how is your blood pressure been at home since you've been taking since these episodes that you've been sick are you taking norvasc

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i am but i keep them on some days because my blood pressure has been running somewhat on the low side

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and right now it's about a hundred and twenty over seventy what how low does it go in the more when you check it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well there are sometimes in the lower nineties

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well let me let me examine you here in a second and we'll think about you know what we need to do especially with your coughing and your shortness of breath okay so nita i'm i'm examining you now let's pretend i did my exam i'm just gon na verbalize some of my findings so i can put it into my chart okay and i'll explain what those things mean so on my exam you've got no jvd there is no swelling in your neck no carotid bruits your lung exam you've got some rails and some rhonchi on on more on the right side i do n't hear any wheezing right now there is some diminished breath sounds in the right side as well on your heart exam you've got two over successolic ejection murmur you've had that in the past that's unchanged from before the rest regular rate and rhythm otherwise your belly exam is nice and soft on your extremity exam you've got one plus nonpitting edema on both of your lower extremities on your on your ankles no calf tenderness no negative homan sign so what does all this mean so basically you've got you know you've got some sort of infection or something going on in your lungs that i can hear right now the rest of your exam is pretty much stable it's unchanged from before so let's talk about what we what we should do about this so for the for the first problem with the shortness of breath the first thing i wan na do is go ahead and get a chest x-ray for you okay you've been coughing up blood i also wan na send you to the emergency department to get some blood testing done i'm worried about a blood clot or something else going on so i wan na get a cat scan of your chest as well so i'm gon na go ahead and refer you to the emergency department i'll call them and and have you head over that way they can get a chest x-ray and a cat scan and some blood work as well and then we'll evaluate why you're having the shortness of breath and why you're coughing up this blood okay and then depending on what they find you may need you know it could be as simple as a pneumonia or it could be something a little bit more serious we may have to get you know start you know depending on what you find we will get the right treatment started any questions about that or can you drive to the emergency department from here or is that okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i can bike no problem i have no question

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright for the second problem for the diabetes since your blood sugar is running a little bit high once you get over this i'm blood sugars running a little bit high once we get you over this hump of the shortness of breath and get this diagnosed i'm gon na go ahead and order another hemoglobin a1c today we will probably increase your dose of metformin from five hundred twice a day to seven fifty twice a day so why do n't we do that and then why do n't we have we'll have the hemoglobin a1c back and then we can once you get over this illness that you're undergoing we'll we'll we'll discuss that more i'll have you come back in about three weeks to just discuss your diabetes and what our treatment plan will be for that okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for the high blood pressure definitely hold off on the norvasc for right now let's figure out what's going what's causing this episode of shortness of breath i want some blood testing done and and once we have that under better control we can reassess your blood pressure so right now hold the norvasc and then we'll have you come back once everything is done okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any other questions or anything we know if we need to talk about today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i'm good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sounds good we'll get you signed out here in a second

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 57-year-old male with a history of diabetes, hypertension, and asthma.
Shortness of breath
The doctor asks about the duration of the patient's symptoms.
The patient has been feeling unwell for about two weeks.
The patient reports waking up with shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the patient is coughing up anything or has leg swelling.
The patient reports coughing up blood.
The doctor asks about the duration of the hemoptysis.
The patient has been coughing up blood for about three weeks.
The doctor asks about the volume of blood produced during coughing.
The patient reports coughing up three to four cups of blood a day.
The doctor asks if the patient is vomiting blood or experiencing bleeding elsewhere.
The patient reports bleeding only when coughing.
The doctor asks about bruising or bleeding from the gums.
The patient denies bruising or bleeding from the gums.
The doctor asks about leg swelling and recent travel history.
The patient reports chronic knee pain without swelling.
The patient's pulse oxygenation is 94 percent.
The doctor asks about the patient's diabetes control and metformin use.
Diabetes
The patient reports poor diabetes control and uses a Dexcom monitor.
The patient reports post-meal blood sugar spikes over 200.
The patient reports morning blood sugar around 150.
Hypertension
The doctor asks about blood pressure control and Norvasc use.
The patient reports taking Norvasc but skipping doses due to low blood pressure readings.
The patient's current blood pressure is 120/70.
The doctor asks how low the patient's blood pressure goes at home.
The patient reports blood pressure readings in the lower 90s.

Physical exam findings: neck supple, no JVD, no carotid bruits; lungs have rales/rhonchi on right, diminished breath sounds on right, no wheezing; heart has 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, regular rate/rhythm; abdomen soft; 1+ nonpitting edema bilateral lower extremities, no calf tenderness, negative Homan's sign.
The doctor orders a chest x-ray, CT scan, and blood work at the ER.
The doctor asks if the patient can drive to the emergency department.
The doctor orders an A1c test and plans to increase metformin to 750 mg twice daily, with a follow-up in three weeks.
The doctor instructs the patient to discontinue Norvasc.

case_description: shortness of breath with hemoptysis; diabetes;hypertension
